nodes.title: Metalness Guide
widgets_values:
METALNESS â€” HOW TO ADJUST

This map is INVERTED ROUGHNESS. Smooth surfaces (low roughness) â†’ bright metalness. Rough surfaces â†’ dark metalness.

â”€â”€ NON-METALLIC MATERIAL (wood, stone, fabric, leather) â”€â”€
Expect dark / black result. That's correct â€” leave as is.
If it looks too bright: set Brightness to -1 or -2.

â”€â”€ MIXED MATERIAL (painted metal, worn / rusted metal) â”€â”€
Smooth shiny patches will appear metallic (bright).
Rough corroded patches will appear non-metallic (dark).
Raise Contrast (3â€“5) to sharpen the boundary between them.

â”€â”€ PURE METAL (steel, chrome, polished aluminium) â”€â”€
Expect mostly bright / white result.
If still too dark: raise Brightness to 2â€“4.
If flat grey with no detail: raise Contrast to 4â€“6.

â”€â”€ COMPLETELY BLACK NO MATTER WHAT â”€â”€
CHORD output uniform high roughness â€” nothing to invert.
Try Brightness 4â€“6 to lift the floor, then Contrast 3â€“5.
For mirror-finish metal consider painting metalness manually.
